text: The bearing of this maxim is very doubtful; we may translate ‘sickness’ instead of ‘infirmity of purpose’.

v. 33: ‘To the drunkard’; literally, ‘to him who is lessened by (or, in respect of) wine’. Elsewhere in this book this verb expresses some deficit in personal qualities. The sense ‘to him who must go without wine’ is admissible, and if it is adopted, the next verse will refer not to immoderate drinking, but to an empty cellar.
